reviews.review_msg:
Josip is the best. Super engaging classes: a combo of group discussions, mini-lectures, videos, etc. so its always interesting but no one is forced to participate. 

Gives incredibly clear assignment guidelines and is a very generous marker if you follow his instructions. Very kind, chill, funny, but super intelligent and an excellent teacher.

Prof Dasovic was great. He does his best to convey stats to social sciences students and is sure to pepper a lot of dad jokes into his lectures! The course content is difficult and the workload is heavy - but he does his best to make sure everyone gets it. He's very available outside of class and more than happy to help if you're having any trouble

I loved having him as a professor. He is super understanding and really cares about his students, he will go out of his way to ensure everyone knows what is going on and will alter exams/midterms if the class needs it. He is super quick to respond to emails and questions. If you put in a smidgen of effort, you will do well in his class.

Although this course was a little on the more difficult side, Professor Dasovic tried his best to make stats understandable for those who werent as well versed. His lectures were well organized and detailed; he even went out of his way to assist students through email or by sending out helpful resources! Overall great Professor

Joseph was an amazing professor!I was skeptical of taking this course with him after reading the reviews, but I am glad I still decided to!Although the course content was difficult to grasp, he tried his best to make sure we understood! I suggest coming to class, since class and lab attendance amounts to 1/4th of your grade (which helps A LOT!).

Have a better attitude that's not "I don't need to learn this so I won't bother" and you'll do fine. Tough grader, but the material is tough so it makes sense. Prof is SUPER available outside of class, so if you get on top of what you don't understand early it will be fine.

Using test averages is not a good reflection of the course overall. Lecture outlines were very detailed, assignments were difficult but mostly straightforward if you attended tutorial; Josip was very often available for office hours and helpful if you had specific questions; TA Alex was also helpful in tutorial. Not too bad of a class if you worked

Do not take him as a prof. Extremely disorganized and all over the place. The final was 30%, the paper was 10%, and an assignment worth 5% all in the last week of the semester. No communication with his students after the final either. The average for the final exam was 58%........

Yes 50 percent of grade was due in last two weeks but final exam was 35, paper 10 (which people had approximately 1 month to work on ) and 5 hwk (hwk25) Marking was very fair, lectures imperitive. mid-term and final heavily lecture based but notes very detailed. Josip presents stats in intuitive way. Made you work for an A; IT's UNI THOUGH RIGHT??

NO knowledge on HOW to teach what he knows. Had an R assignment analytic paper & the final all due within the same week. To sum it up, 50% of our mark due in the last week of the semester. Stops communicating & neglects students towards the end of semester when half of their grade is on the line. AVOID AT ALL COSTS. Tough marker, HEAVY course load

Seems knowledgeable on the course content if you go to office hours but doesn't communicate expectations very clearly. Midterm average was below 50 (without the curve) and he only gave us the study guide for the final 2 days in advance. HW assignments took very long but average on them wasn't bad. Overall okay but don't take with more than 9 cred.

Professor Dasovic is a super funny prof and understands that us IS kids are NOT meant to take stats and research. Although lectures are pretty boring he gives you pretty clear cut definitions of all the terms you will need to know for the midterm and final. Expect lots of research projects throughout the semester.

Exams are your grade booster for this class. They are solely based on his lectures so make sure you attend them. His assignments are hell in order to survive them make sure you form a study group. Once you have a study group setup you should be okay. This class can be a GPA booster if you just put time and effort into it. Take only taking 3 classes

Unorganized & assignments were too long. Does provide help but replies r vague & by end of semester was unhelpfulignored students. Definitely make a study group becuz will take group effort to finish assignments. However exams were not too difficult just study lecture notes altho textbook provides examples. Try to get into TA tutorial & not his.
He doesn't just give answers away, he teaches you to think.

Ok as person he really nice.however, as a prof, his exam long 12-14 short answers + 2 essays (u have to know both books very well), his marking scale so high, B- start from 76. I wouldn't take another class with him nor recommend him for IS210

Engaging, great lecturer, and if you go to his office hours, very helpful. If you go out of your way to contact him, he's willing to go out of his way to help you. While he could be better organised, I feel like that's just because this was his first semester back after taking a break from teaching for a while. Loved taking IS 240 & IS 210 with him

Very engaging, very knowledgeable professor. He doesn't tend to pull punches, he will challenge you, but not to overload. Smaller class size where conversation is encouraged is where his teaching style fits best. Not everyone clicks with his sense of humour, taken out of context he can be a source of politically incorrect memes.

For an extremely boring subject I had no interest in, Dasovic makes it better than tolerable. Some people react negatively to his style, he can bounce around a little, I personally like it. He tries hard to keep things more engaging, always answers questions, and is usually quick with email responses. The course itself = awful, Dasovic = great.

The fact negative comments are referring to him as a "teacher" just shows the intelligence level of people taking his classes. Dasovic is an amazing PROFESSOR. He wants you to do well and is incredibly helpful. Exams are very very fair and had the potential to be harder. Possible to get an A+.

People either love him or hate him... If you like teachers who are helpful and can guide you through material, DO NOT take his class. If you like teachers who are not totally clueless on the material they're teaching DO NOT take his class. You have been warned.

Not a very helpful teacher. Comes to class unprepared and has admitted to not knowing that much about 'R' (program he's teaching us to use). Showed up to our midterm 10 minutes late and half awake. If coding is not your thing and/or you'd much prefer a teacher who can actually guide you and help you understand the material, do not take his courses.

I have this prof for two of my classes and am so thankful. He is incredibly helpful, very fair and passionate about what he teaches. He doesn't spoon feed you, but he challenges you to think for yourself whilst helping you along the way. Midterm was very fair and quite easy. Put in the effort and you will be satisfied.

Absolutely Useless. Not only does he not come prepared to Labs/Tutorials/Sections, but he is incredibly unhelpful in providing even a shred of guidance. Condescending to his students, he is often rude and impatient, and tries to shift blame to his students even though it is he who cannot understand what he is trying to teach. AVOID AT ALL COSTS.
Very passionate about what he teaches, but doesn't lecture as much as one would like to see. Reading was way too much. He's funny though, and charming

Absolutely fantastic prof - highly recommended. He provides notes, but make sure to go to the lectures - he's entertaining and explains things beyond the notes, which may help you understand things more.

Don't know what the person below is talking about. The amount of readings was huge, felt like a grad class. Assignment topics were incomprehensible- not sure what he wanted, and he did not give enough time to complete them. Hard to put in genuine effort when all you're trying to do is get assignments done on time.A good academic,but def not a prof.

Good reasonable prof w a sense of humour. Tries to interest students and provides numerous anecdotes and videos. Lack of coordination with TA though. Cut huge chunks of initial syllabus and switched readings,such that one course book (purchased) was not used at all. Overall, fair. Not stellar, but also no huge complaints.
